name: Gray Vogel
response: I struggle with knowing when to stop collecting data because there is always pressure for one more round. Leadership asked for a dashboard, but interviews showed people needed reminders, not charts.

name: Casey Hughes
response: Stakeholders wanted velocity, but the real risk was in unstated assumptions about user behavior. The most useful moment was when a participant walked us through a workaround step by step.

name: Cameron Fisher
response: Synthesis dragged because everyone wanted to tag transcripts differently, so we aligned on a lightweight codebook first. We shared a two-minute clip from a session and the engineering conversation finally shifted.

response: I have been experimenting with shorter readouts because long decks get skimmed and forgotten. I document assumptions at kickoff so we can revisit them when results surprise us.

name: Vince Okonkwo
response: People mixed up two labels that looked similar on mobile, which pushed us to tighten the design system guidance. We prioritized fixing language before adding new functionality, which reduced support load.

response: Customer support scripts contradicted the UI in places, which showed up as repeated tickets. I write a one-page brief for busy partners and attach the appendix for those who want depth.

response: Accessibility came up when we watched assistive tech users navigate flows we thought were straightforward. Since then, I push for a shared glossary so design and content stay aligned.
